claim: Birkhoff (G. D.) 1911, 'General theory of linear difference equations', Trans. Amer. Math. Soc. 12 (1911), 243-284, exists and addresses the regular and integer-rank irregular cases of linear difference equations at infinity.

Adams (C. R.) 1928, 'On the irregular cases of the linear ordinary difference equation', Trans. Amer. Math. Soc. 30 no.3 (1928), 507-541, exists and gives the complete formal-series treatment for the order-2 irregular linear difference equation.

Birkhoff & Trjitzinsky 1933, 'Analytic theory of singular difference equations', Acta Mathematica 60 (1933), 1-89, exists and is the principal reference for formal-series existence and formal Stokes-classification theorems for linear difference operators at infinity, including the fractional-rank case.

Turrittin 1955, 'Convergent solutions of ordinary linear homogeneous difference equations in the neighborhood of an irregular singular point', Acta Mathematica 93 (1955), 27-66, exists and provides the first rigorous convergent-solution theorem for the regular-singular irregular-difference-equation case.

Wasow 1965, 'Asymptotic Expansions for Ordinary Differential Equations', Wiley/Interscience, exists and contains in Chapter X the canonical secondary treatment of B-T 1933 with a polynomial-coefficient refinement (sec. X.3) that underwrites the literature upper bound psi_upper(d) = 2d in Q3 of this Phase-1.

B-T 1933, Theorem 1 (formal-series existence): for an order-n linear difference operator with rational coefficients at infinity with Newton-polygon slopes sigma_1 < ... < sigma_r, there exist a positive integer q and n formal solutions of canonical form exp(P_j(x^(1/q))) x^rho_j hat-S_j(x^(-1/q)). [paraphrased from Wasow 1965 sec X.2 and Immink 1984 sec 1.3; primary-source quotation pending operator institutional access].

B-T 1933, Theorem 2 (formal Stokes-classification): two formal solutions of B-T canonical form belong to the same Stokes datum iff they agree in (P_j, rho_j, hat-S_j) elementwise; the collection (P_j, rho_j, hat-S_j) is a complete formal invariant. [paraphrased from Wasow 1965 sec X.3 and Wimp-Zeilberger 1985 sec 2; primary-source quotation pending].

Turrittin 1955, Theorem A (regular-singular convergent-solutions): in the regular-singular Newton-polygon stratum at infinity, the formal series of B-T 1933 is the asymptotic expansion of a genuine analytic solution on a sector, with formal-to-analytic correspondence rigid up to Stokes multipliers. [paraphrased; Acta Math 93 (1955), 27-66, primary-source verification pending].

Wasow 1965 sec X.3 (polynomial-coefficient refinement): for a three-term linear difference equation with polynomial coefficients of degree exactly d at the leading and trailing ends, the maximum formal Newton-polygon slope at infinity in the SIARC-matching normalisation is bounded above by 2d. [paraphrased from Wasow 1965; primary-source verification pending; under the Wasow-normalisation reading this gives psi_upper(d) = 2d].

Immink 1984 LNM 1085 sec II.3 (Borel-1 summability for rank-1 difference equations): in the rank-1 irregular Newton-polygon stratum at infinity, the formal-series solutions of B-T 1933 are Borel-1 summable, hence give the asymptotic expansion of a genuine analytic solution on a sector with no formal-to-analytic gap. [paraphrased from Immink LNM 1085; primary-source verification pending].

Q3 derivation: psi_lower(d) = d under the Wasow-normalisation reading. Derivation: Birkhoff 1911 Thm 4 gives slope sigma >= d/n for a generic order-n linear difference equation with polynomial coefficients of degree <= d; for n=2 this is sigma >= d/2 in Birkhoff's normalisation, translating to A >= d in the SIARC normalisation under the Wasow match.

Q3 derivation: psi_upper(d) = 2d under the Wasow-normalisation reading. Derivation: Wasow 1965 sec X.3 polynomial-coefficient refinement bounds the maximum formal Newton-polygon slope at infinity by 2d in the SIARC-matching normalisation; sectorial-analytic upgrade Turrittin 1955 (regular-singular) / Immink 1984 (irregular Borel-1 summable) preserves the slope value, yielding A <= 2d.

Q3 gap-type classification: GAP_TYPE_C under the Wasow-normalisation reading. Derivation: psi_lower(d) = d < 2d = psi_upper(d) for all d >= 1; B4 conjectures A = 2d at the literature upper bound; Phase-2 task is to lift psi_lower from d to 2d. Provenance: gap_proposition_for_d_ge_3.md sec 4.

Q3 normalisation-match dependency: the GAP_TYPE_C classification depends on the Wasow-normalisation reading (SIARC A corresponds to Wasow's sigma). If the primary-source reading of B-T 1933 / Adams 1928 instead matches Adams's 2*sigma, the bracket becomes [d/2, d] and B4 becomes GAP_TYPE_A with A = 2d outside the literature upper bound, which is a major Phase-2 halt condition. Provenance: gap_proposition_for_d_ge_3.md sec 3-4 caveats.

Q4 triangulation: SIARC empirical record E1 (110/110 cubic+quartic families with A_fit within 1e-2 of 2d at dps>=24) narrows A at d in {3,4} to a sub-percent neighbourhood of 2d, inside the literature bracket [d, 2d] of Q3 GAP_TYPE_C, supporting the Phase-2 lower-bound lift. E1 does NOT establish A = 2d rigorously at any d.

Q4 triangulation: SIARC empirical record E2 (PCF2-SESSION-T2 Petersson-discriminant correlation rho=+0.638 at n=50, p_Bonf=8.6e-6) is orthogonal to Q3's leading-A bracket; E2 acts on sub-leading delta = A_fit - 6, not on A directly. E2 is consistent with Phase-2 sub-claim P2.3 (sectorial uniformity) and does NOT establish any sub-leading closed-form for delta.

Q4 triangulation: Theory-Fleet H4 is an unexecuted theoretical-prediction artefact (median Ecalle resurgence, 5000 coefficients at dps 250, central forecast 40 digits of the V_quad alien amplitude); E3 is orthogonal to Q3's bracket on A and rules out nothing empirically. The rigorous backbone of E3 is Costin 1998 + Loday-Richaud 2016, in which median half-Stokes prescription is theorem-grade for rank-1 resurgence problems.
